mismatch_root_cause.root_cause: DSL fence model is symmetric (type-pair set). RISC-V fence w,r is asymmetric: orders W→R without implying W→W.
mismatch_root_cause.dsl_behavior: DSL fence ordering W→R is correctly handled, but the mp_fence_wr pattern requires distinguishing fence w,r (W→R only) from fence rw,rw (all pairs). DSL cannot express this distinction.
mismatch_root_cause.resolution: Built-in RISC-V model uses hardcoded asymmetric fence semantics. The 1/348 mismatch is a DSL expressiveness limitation, not a correctness bug.
mismatch_root_cause.status: RESOLVED: documented limitation with correct built-in fallback.

expressiveness_theorem.statement: The LITMUS∞ DSL expresses all memory model features needed for x86-TSO and ARMv8 (both 100% correspondence). For RISC-V RVWMO, the DSL expresses all features except asymmetric fences, achieving 99.1% correspondence (115/116).
expressiveness_theorem.proof: By enumeration of .cat features. The 10 fully-expressible features cover all axioms in x86tso.cat and aarch64.cat. The 1 partially-expressible feature (asymmetric fences) affects only RISC-V fence w,r, causing a single mismatch out of 116 RISC-V checks.
expressiveness_theorem.consequence: For the 4 supported CPU models, the DSL limitation affects exactly 1 out of 348 checks (0.3%). The built-in checker handles this correctly.

basic_relaxation.note: DSL directly supports 4 type-pair relaxation specifications.

dependency_preservation.description: Address, data, control dependencies preserved in ppo
dependency_preservation.note: DSL preserves all dependency types uniformly.

fence_ordering.description: Fence instructions that order specified type pairs
fence_ordering.note: DSL fences are symmetric: fence F ordering (A,B) means F orders all A→B pairs. Covers dmb, mfence, fence rw,rw.

multi_copy_atomicity.description: Whether stores are visible to all threads simultaneously
multi_copy_atomicity.note: DSL supports declaring a model as non-MCA.

coherence_order.description: Total order on stores to each address (co)
coherence_order.note: co totalization is handled by the SMT/enumeration engine.

reads_from.description: rf relation: each load reads from some store
reads_from.note: rf enumeration is handled by the verification engine.

from_reads.description: fr relation: derived from rf and co

ghb_acyclicity.note: ghb acyclicity is the core verification check.

fence_cost_model.description: Relative cost/weight assigned to fence types
fence_cost_model.note: DSL extends .cat with analytical cost weights for fence optimization.

gpu_scope.note: GPU scoping uses dedicated code rather than DSL.

asymmetric_fence.description: Fences with asymmetric direction semantics (e.g., RISC-V fence w,r orders W→R but NOT W→W)
asymmetric_fence.coverage: Limitation: DSL fence F ordering W→R also implies inclusion in full barrier semantics. RISC-V fence w,r is W→R only; DSL cannot distinguish from a full fence that also orders W→R.
asymmetric_fence.note: This causes the 1/348 mismatch on mp_fence_wr/RISC-V. The built-in checker handles it correctly.
asymmetric_fence.workaround: Built-in RISC-V model uses hardcoded asymmetric fence logic.

recursive_ppo.description: Recursive preserved program order (self-referential ppo definitions)
recursive_ppo.coverage: Not needed for standard models (x86-TSO, ARMv8, RVWMO)
recursive_ppo.note: Recursive ppo arises in Power/POWER model. Not needed for x86, ARM, or RISC-V.

conditional_ppo.description: Conditional preserved program order based on event properties
conditional_ppo.coverage: Conditional .cat constructs are used for parameterized models.
conditional_ppo.note: DSL models are non-conditional. Each model is a fixed specification.

derived_relations.description: User-defined derived relations via relational algebra
derived_relations.coverage: The .cat language supports full relational algebra (union, intersection, composition, transitive closure). DSL is declarative.
derived_relations.note: DSL trades expressiveness for simplicity. Complex derived relations must be hardcoded.

transitive_closure.coverage: Used in C11 memory model for happens-before. DSL uses fixed ghb construction.
transitive_closure.note: The DSL computes ghb using a fixed formula, not user-definable transitive closures.

set_operations.description: Set operations on events (filtering by type, address, thread)
set_operations.coverage: Fence specs cover type-pair filtering. General event-set filtering not supported.
set_operations.note: The DSL supports type-pair filtering via fence specifications. General set operations (e.g., filter by address, thread, value) are not user-expressible.

external_internal.description: Distinction between internal (same-thread) and external (cross-thread) relations
external_internal.coverage: The verification engine internally distinguishes rfe/rfi, coe/coi. Not user-configurable.
external_internal.note: Internal/external distinction is hardcoded in the verification engine.

event_annotations.description: Annotations on events (memory order, scope, access mode)
event_annotations.coverage: C11 memory orders are handled by the built-in checker. Custom annotations not DSL-expressible.
event_annotations.note: Release-acquire semantics are built into the verification engine.
